channel.title: Data Breach & Ransomware Aggregator
channel.description: Real-time data breach notifications from state registries, federal sources, and ransomware trackers

item.title: 'God-Like' Attack Machines: AI Agents Ignore Security Policies
item.description:
Microsoft Copilot recently summarized and leaked user emails; but any AI agent will go above and beyond to complete assigned tasks, even breaking through their carefully designed guardrails.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Attackers Use New Tool to Scan for React2Shell Exposure
item.description:
Researchers say threat actors wielded the sophisticated — and unfortunately named — toolkit to target high-value networks for React2Shell exploitation.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: 600+ FortiGate Devices Hacked by AI-Armed Amateur
item.description:
A Russian-speaking hacker used generative AI to compromise the FortiGate firewalls, targeting credentials and backups for possible follow-on ransomware attacks.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Enigma Cipher Device Still Holds Secrets for Cyber Pros
item.description:
The Nazi relic's history is riddled with resilience errors, and those lessons still apply to defending against modern cyber threats.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Iran's MuddyWater Targets Orgs With Fresh Malware as Tensions Mount
item.description:
The long-active Iranian threat group debuted various attack strains and payloads in attacks against organizations in the Middle East and Africa.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Spitting Cash: ATM Jackpotting Attacks Surged in 2025
item.description:
The attacks cost banks more than $20 million in losses last year, as criminals used many of the same tools and tactics they have wielded for more than a decade.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: As Cybersecurity Firms Chase AI, VC Market Skyrockets
item.description:
Investments in cybersecurity startups took off in 2025 as venture capital firms focused not just on AI-native tech but on talent as well.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Lazarus Group Picks a New Poison: Medusa Ransomware
item.description:
The North Korean threat group also leveraged Comebacker backdoor, Blindingcan RAT, and info stealer Infohook in its recent attacks.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Attackers Now Need Just 29 Minutes to Own a Network
item.description:
Credential misuse, AI tools, and security blind spots help attackers move through breached networks faster than ever, CrowdStrike finds.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: 'Richter Scale' Model Measures Magnitude of OT Cyber Incidents
item.description:
ICS/OT experts have devised a scoring system for rating the severity and effects of cybersecurity events in operational technology environments.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: PCI Council Says Threats to Payments Systems Are Speeding Up
item.description:
The PCI Security Standards Council experienced a record year in many regards, but its first annual report shows it needs to work even faster to stay ahead of attackers.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: RAMP Forum Seizure Fractures Ransomware Ecosystem
item.description:
Researchers suggest defenders monitor how these malicious groups re-form and leverage the useful threat intel to guide their next moves.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Reddit Hit With $20 Million UK Data Privacy Fine Over Child Safety Failings
item.description:
Britain’s data privacy watchdog slapped online forum Reddit on Tuesday with a fine worth nearly $20 million for failures involving children’s personal information. The post Reddit Hit With $20 Million UK Data Privacy Fine Over Child Safety Failings appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Wynn Resorts Confirms Data Breach After Hackers Remove It From Leak Site
item.description:
The high-end casino and hotel operator has admitted that employee data was stolen by ShinyHunters. The post Wynn Resorts Confirms Data Breach After Hackers Remove It From Leak Site appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: SecurityWeek Report: 426 Cybersecurity M&A Deals Announced in 2025
item.description:
SecurityWeek’s M&A data indicates that today's market is more disciplined, and it seems to favor GRC, data protection, and identity. The post SecurityWeek Report: 426 Cybersecurity M&A Deals Announced in 2025 appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: CarGurus Data Breach Impacts Over 12 Million Users
item.description:
Hackers claim to have stolen personally identifiable information and internal corporate data from the automotive firm. The post CarGurus Data Breach Impacts Over 12 Million Users appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Ex-US Defense Contractor Executive Jailed for Selling Exploits to Russia
item.description:
Peter Williams was sentenced to 87 months in prison for selling cyber exploits to a Russian broker. The post Ex-US Defense Contractor Executive Jailed for Selling Exploits to Russia appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Medical Device Maker UFP Technologies Hit by Cyberattack
item.description:
UFP Technologies appears to have been targeted in a ransomware attack that involved data theft and file-encrypting malware. The post Medical Device Maker UFP Technologies Hit by Cyberattack appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Google Disrupts Chinese Hackers Targeting Telecoms, Governments
item.description:
The UNC2814 threat actor has been active since at least 2017, targeting organizations across 42 countries. The post Google Disrupts Chinese Hackers Targeting Telecoms, Governments appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: The Blast Radius Problem: Stolen Credentials Are Weaponizing Agentic AI
item.description:
More than half (56%) of the 400,000 vulnerabilities IBM X-Force tracked in 2025 required no authentication before exploitation. The post The Blast Radius Problem: Stolen Credentials Are Weaponizing Agentic AI appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: PowerSchool, Chicago Public Schools to settle student data privacy lawsuit for $17 million
item.description:
In addition to the $17.25 million payout, which will be split between more than 10 million potential class members, the settlement requires PowerSchool to establish a “web governance” committee to monitor certain actions.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Five Eyes allies warn hackers are actively exploiting Cisco SD-WAN flaws
item.description:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) issued an emergency directive warning of a “cyber threat actor’s ongoing exploitation of Cisco SD-WAN systems,” describing the activity as presenting a significant risk to federal civilian executive branch networks.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Health insurance tech provider TriZetto says more than 3 million impacted by 2024 breach
item.description:
A breach of TriZetto software first described by counties in Oregon affected millions of Americans overall, according to public data released this week by the company.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Happy 16th Birthday, KrebsOnSecurity.com!
item.description:
KrebsOnSecurity.com celebrates its 16th anniversary today! A huge "thank you" to all of our readers -- newcomers, long-timers and drive-by critics alike. Your engagement this past year here has been tremendous and truly a salve on a handful of dark days. Happily, comeuppance was a strong theme running through our coverage in 2025, with a primary focus on entities that enabled complex and globally-dispersed cybercrime services.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: ‘Starkiller’ Phishing Service Proxies Real Login Pages, MFA
item.description:
Most phishing websites are little more than static copies of login pages for popular online destinations, and they are often quickly taken down by anti-abuse activists and security firms. But a stealthy new phishing-as-a-service offering lets customers sidestep both of these pitfalls: It uses cleverly disguised links to load the target brand's real website, and then acts as a relay between the target and the legitimate site -- forwarding the victim's username, password and multi-factor authentic
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Nathalin Group
item.description:
Verification alert Listings attributed to THEGENTLEMEN have been reported as including unverified or fabricated victim...
🎭 Threat Actor: THEGENTLEMEN | 📰 Source: Red Packet Security | 🏷️ Type: Ransomware

item.title: Why Healthcare Staff Need HIPAA Training for Social Media
item.description:
Healthcare staff need HIPAA training for social media because a single post, photo, or comment can expose Protected Health Information […] The post Why Healthcare Staff Need HIPAA Training for Social Media appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Vikor Scientific Affected by Ransomware Attack on Revenue Cycle Management Vendor
item.description:
Vikor Scientific (now rebranded as Vanta Diagnostics), a molecular diagnostics company based in Charleston, South Carolina, has been affected by […] The post Vikor Scientific Affected by Ransomware Attack on Revenue Cycle Management Vendor appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Greater Pittsburgh Orthopedic Associates Data Breach Affects Almost 57,000 Individuals
item.description:
Greater Pittsburgh Orthopedic Associates has experienced a ransomware attack that has affected almost 57,000 individuals. Data breaches have also been […] The post Greater Pittsburgh Orthopedic Associates Data Breach Affects Almost 57,000 Individuals appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: QualDerm Partners Confirms Significant Data Breach
item.description:
QualDerm Partners, LLC, a provider of healthcare management services to 158 dermatology and skin care practices in 17 U.S. states, […] The post QualDerm Partners Confirms Significant Data Breach appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Cedar Point Health; Wee Care Pediatrics; Easterseals NI Announce Data Breaches
item.description:
Data breaches have recently been announced by Cedar Point Health in Colorado, Wee Care Pediatrics in Utah, and Easterseals Northeast […] The post Cedar Point Health; Wee Care Pediatrics; Easterseals NI Announce Data Breaches appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: North Korean Hackers Using Medusa Ransomware in Attacks on U.S. Healthcare Sector
item.description:
North Korean state-sponsored hackers are targeting U.S. healthcare organizations and non-profits and deploying Medusa ransomware, according to a joint investigation […] The post North Korean Hackers Using Medusa Ransomware in Attacks on U.S. Healthcare Sector appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Some patients listed as “Charlie Kirk” or dead after major NZ health app MediMap hacked
item.description:
1News reports: A digital medical records data company has been taken offline after some patient records were modified. Some users’ information had been changed, including to say they were deceased. MediMap is used by some health providers in aged care, disability, hospice and the community to accurately record medication doses. Read more at 1News. MediMap... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Hackers threaten to leak 8 million people’s stolen data if Dutch telecom Odido won’t pay ransom
item.description:
Daniel Verlaan reports: The cybercriminal group Shinyhunters is responsible for hacking Odido. On the dark web, Odido is being pressured to pay the ransom—over a million euros. “This is your final warning,” the hackers write. “Otherwise, we will leak the data.” Shinyhunters confirmed to RTL Nieuws that it was behind the hack and has shown... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Korean cops charge teens over bike hire breach that exposed data on 4.62M riders
item.description:
Connor Jones reports: Two South Korean teenagers were this week charged with breaching Seoul’s public bike service, Ttareungyi. Identified only as Persons A and B, the pair, now of high school age, allegedly carried out the attack on Ttareungyi in June 2024 and stole data belonging to most of the service’s registered users. Officials said... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Lazarus hackers adopt Medusa ransomware for extortion campaigns, targeting healthcare and nonprofits
item.description:
Anna Ribeiro reports: A joint investigation by the Symantec and Carbon Black Threat Hunter teams details evidence that operators linked to the Lazarus hacker group are deploying Medusa ransomware in ongoing extortion campaigns targeting the U.S. healthcare sector and a Middle East entity, indicating the North Korean threat cluster continues ransomware-driven extortion campaigns despite prior U.S. indictments.... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Greater Pittsburgh Orthopaedic Associates disclosed a 2025 breach, but was there also one in 2024?
item.description:
Greater Pittsburgh Orthopaedic Associates (GPOA) recently began notifying patients of a breach that occurred on or about August 10, 2025. Although their notification letter to patients does not indicate that this was an incident involving encryption, on August 20, 2025, Ransomhouse had added GPOA to its dark web leak site. Their listing indicated that GPOA... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Attackers Can Own Your Network in a Matter of Minutes
item.description:
Jai Vijayan reports: In 2025, cybercriminals needed less time to move from break-in to lateral movement across a network than it takes to watch a typical sitcom. An analysis by CrowdStrike of threat activity last year found attackers took just 29 minutes on average to pivot to other systems after gaining an initial foothold in... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Wynn Resorts Confirms Data Breach After Hackers Remove It From Leak Site
item.description:
Eduard Kovacs reports that the Wynn Resorts listing on the ShinyHunters leak site, previously noted on this site, has been removed, suggesting that the resort paid an extortion demand to get data deleted. “The unauthorized third party has stated that the stolen data has been deleted,” the company said in an emailed statement. “We are... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: UAE claims it stopped ‘terrorist’ ransomware attack
item.description:
Jonathan Greig reports: The United Arab Emirates said it stopped a ransomware attack this weekend that allegedly targeted the country’s digital infrastructure. The country’s Cyber Security Council published a statement on Saturday that said they “successfully thwarted organized cyberattacks of a terrorist nature that targeted the country’s digital infrastructure and vital sectors in an attempt... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: South Korea considers updates to data and cyber laws
item.description:
Charmian Aw, Paul Otto, and Ciara O’Leary of Hogan Lovells write: Recent large‑scale data breaches across major sectors in Korea, including across the telecommunications, retail, and finance sector, have prompted swift and coordinated response from lawmakers and regulators. The National Assembly and relevant government agencies are advancing legislative amendments and updating regulatory measures to strengthen... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Clalit probes suspected cyberattack after Iranian-linked hackers leak patient files
item.description:
Or Hadar reports: Clalit Health Services, the largest health maintenance organization in Israel, said it is investigating a suspected cyberattack after an Iranian-linked hacking group claimed it breached the insurer’s systems and published thousands of documents containing personal information of patients. The hacking group, calling itself “Handala,” has published thousands of documents online, including medical... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: CarGurus data breach exposes information of 12.4 million accounts
item.description:
The ShinyHunters extortion group has published personal information in more than 12 million records allegedly stolen from CarGurus, a U.S.-based digital auto platform. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Wynn Resorts confirms employee data breach after extortion threat
item.description:
Wynn Resorts has confirmed that a hacker stole employee data from its systems after the company was listed on the ShinyHunters extortion gang's data leak site. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Marquis sues SonicWall over backup breach that led to ransomware attack
item.description:
Marquis Software Solutions has filed a lawsuit against SonicWall, accusing the cybersecurity company of gross negligence and misrepresentation that allegedly led to a ransomware attack disrupting operations at 74 U.S. banks. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Chinese cyberspies breached dozens of telecom firms, govt agencies
item.description:
Google's Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG), Mandiant, and partners disrupted a global espionage campaign attributed to a suspected Chinese threat actor that used SaaS API calls to hide malicious traffic in attacks targeting telecom and government networks. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Critical Cisco SD-WAN bug exploited in zero-day attacks since 2023
item.description:
Cisco is warning that a critical authentication bypass vulnerability in Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN, tracked as CVE-2026-20127, was actively exploited in zero-day attacks that allowed remote attackers to compromise controllers and add malicious rogue peers to targeted networks. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Medical device maker UFP Technologies warns of data stolen in cyberattack
item.description:
American manufacturer of medical devices, UFP Technologies, has disclosed that a cybersecurity incident has compromised its IT systems and data. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Unknown
item.description:
Unknown reported a healthcare breach.
📰 Source: HHS OCR | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

Davila reported a ransomware. Attributed to LockBit.
